name: Jordan Mason
headline: Physical early-down hammer with handcuff upside behind Aaron Jones
body:
Mason proved his first Minnesota season was no fluke, churning out 758 yards on 159 carries at 4.77 per tote with six scores across 16 games, finishing as the team's leading rusher despite a 42.8% snap share. The profile is built on contact balance: 2.42 yards after contact per attempt, 13 broken tackles, and a 13.84% explosive-run rate. His -0.059 EPA per play and modest 16-target receiving role cap the ceiling, but 30 red-zone opportunities and seven goal-line carries point to durable touchdown equity.

He enters 2026 as the early-down grinder in a timeshare with Aaron Jones, who handles passing downs. That setup makes Mason a touchdown-dependent flex with genuine RB2 upside the moment Jones misses time. With an ADP near RB42 and goal-line workload baked in, he's a sturdy late-round value whose scoring profile could spike if Minnesota's offense moves the chains efficiently.
